dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, something is wrong with {target_npc}. Deeply, unsettlingly wrong.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Three days ago they were perfectly normal. Then they wandered into the woods {location_hint} and came back... different. Speaking in fragments, drawing strange symbols on the walls, refusing to eat. The alchemist examined them and found traces of {quest_item} on their skin: a substance that shouldn't exist naturally in this region. Whatever they encountered out there changed them. I need someone to retrace their steps and find out what happened before it spreads.
dialogueChunks.outro: I've seen illness and I've seen grief. This is neither. Something out there broke their mind, and I need to know what.

playerResponses.text: I'll follow their trail and find the source. Keep them safe until I return.
Strange substances and broken minds? This feels dangerous. I'll look into it carefully.
Cursed territory and madness? I'm not touching this.

dialogueChunks.intro: Careful where you step, {player_name}. See those markings on the ground? {target_npc} has been carving them all night.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: It started after the alchemist brewed a new tonic using {quest_item} from an unknown source. Three people drank it: two recovered after a few hours of confusion. {target_npc} didn't. They've been speaking to things that aren't there, claiming something in the water at {location} is alive and whispering to them. The alchemist is terrified: says the {quest_item} they used was contaminated with something ancient. The original batch came from a cave {location_hint} where the {enemy_type} have been unusually active.
dialogueChunks.outro: We need a sample of whatever contaminated that batch. Without it, the alchemist can't brew an antidote and {target_npc} may never come back to us.

playerResponses.text: Contaminated ingredients and whispers in the water? I'll find the source.
If the alchemist caused this, the alchemist should fix it. But I'll get the sample.
Ancient contamination and cave creatures? I value my sanity too much.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I need your help. The settlement is frightened and I don't blame them.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: People in {location} have been having the same nightmare for a week: vivid images of a crumbling tower {location_hint} and a voice demanding {quest_item}. Most shake it off by morning, but {target_npc} hasn't slept in three days. They wander the streets at dawn, eyes glazed, muttering directions to a place they've never been. The alchemist brewed a sleeping draft but {target_npc} fought it off like they were possessed. Whatever is causing this, it's centered on that tower.
dialogueChunks.outro: I don't believe in curses, {player_name}. But I believe in what I'm seeing. And what I'm seeing terrifies me.

playerResponses.text: Shared nightmares and a crumbling tower? I'll investigate. Stay close to {target_npc}.
This sounds like more than a bad dream. I'll go, but I need to know everything you know.
Cursed towers and possessions? Find a priest, not an adventurer.

dialogueChunks.intro: Don't be alarmed by the barricaded house, {player_name}. That's... that's where we're keeping {target_npc}.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: They used to be the kindest soul in {location}. Then they found something in the old quarry {location_hint}: some kind of artifact covered in {quest_item} residue. Since they brought it back, they've been violent, paranoid, attacking anyone who comes close. The alchemist says the residue has properties unlike anything in their books. They need a fresh sample from the quarry to study, but the {enemy_type} have moved into the tunnels since {target_npc} disturbed whatever was down there.
dialogueChunks.outro: We've locked them in for their own safety. But every hour their eyes look a little less like a person's. Please hurry.

playerResponses.text: An artifact that drives people mad? I'll get the sample and bring back answers.
If there's something dangerous in that quarry, someone should have sealed it long ago. I'll go.
Whatever turned them violent could do the same to me. I'll pass.
